PI 28

Maintainability Enhancements

Why It's Important
01
Tech Debt Reduction
Restructures revenue-management-be-custom to hexagonal architecture, improving maintainability and reducing long-term technical debt across teams.
02
Critical Upgrades
Implements crucial technology upgrades including Java 25, Spring Boot 4.x, and Oracle 26ai to keep the platform secure and performant.
03
Configuration Management
Introduces Config Items Management tool for safe packaging and migrating configurations across environments without risk.
Key Epics & Features
Hexagonal Architecture Refactor
Restructure revenue-management-be-custom to clean hexagonal architecture with improved separation of concerns, enhanced testability, and reduced coupling across domain boundaries.
Java 25 Upgrade
Migrate codebase to Java 25 to leverage latest language features, virtual threads, pattern matching, and significant performance improvements for tax operations.
Spring Boot 4.x.x Migration
Upgrade Spring Boot framework to latest version with enhanced observability, improved dependency management, native compilation support, and critical security patches.
Oracle 26ai Database
Migrate to Oracle 26ai to harness AI-powered optimization, advanced analytics, and improved query performance for high-volume tax transaction processing.
Tech Debt Across Teams
Systematic UI improvements and technical debt reduction initiatives spanning ATH, EOS, SPA, HEL, and DAID teams for long-term platform sustainability.
Config Items Management
Build dedicated tool for safely packaging, versioning, and migrating configurations across development, staging, and production environments without manual intervention.

agenticCustomInput:
Generate a slide for each  "initiative" found under the "initiative group" called "Core Revenue Management" in "Estimates.xlsx".
"Total hours" value for the "initiative" can be found in the "Estimates.xlsx" where the value is in the "Initiative Total Hours" column.

Dont halucinate numbers. If you cannot find or calculate them, Write [NO DATA].
agenticJsonResponse:
{"slides":{"slide_1":{"instances":[{"auto_div_header":"
PI 28
Feature Catalog · Roadmap Initiative
Billing, Assessments and Installments
Core Revenue Management Capabilities · Solon Tax 2026
1120
Total Hours
2
Epics
GRC
Requester
Netcompany
","auto_div_body":"
Why It's Important
01
Configuration Alignment
By enabling admins to configure separate bill segment types for source and target segments in the Transfer Amount process, we ensure alignment with existing, predefined bill segment configurations used across other procedures.
02
Reduced Duplication
Reduces configuration duplication, minimizes manual corrections, and improves consistency across revenue types and bill segment categories.
03
Ad-hoc Obligation Management
Enables users to create multiple payment obligation actions under ad-hoc obligations as installments, following the structure of non ad-hoc obligations with installments for Inheritance tax and legacy data migration processes.
Key Epics & Features
Transfer Amount - Enhancement on configuration of Bill segment types
As an admin, configure the bill segment types generated by the Transfer Amount process to align with existing, predefined bill segment configurations. Includes new Alternative Target Bill segment type field and relabeling of existing field as Default Bill segment type with mandatory validation and tooltip guidance.
Add and Maintain Ad-hoc Obligations – Pay Instalment Actions and Categories
Enable users and admins to create multiple payment obligation actions under ad-hoc obligations as installments. Users can add Filing and multiple Payment Actions flagged as Installments, define categories (Preliminary, Original), set due dates, and view comprehensive lists with sorting and detail updates.
Bill Segment Type Configuration Mechanism
Default bill segment type is mandatory and applies to source bill segment. Alternative Target Bill segment type applies to target bill segment. If Alternative Target Bill segment type is not filled, Default bill segment type is used for target bill segment.
Ad-hoc Obligation Action Management
Users can see lists of pay instalment actions with identifying information including action category, action type, reference number, due date, amount, and action status. Users can create manually, sort lists, and open actions to view details and update as needed.
Advanced Installment Creation Rules
Predefined rules enable users to create multiple installments by adding parameters such as start date, due date, number of installments, duration between installments, action type, and grace date margin. Admins can define rules in revenue type configuration.
Non-Functional Requirements
All solutions must maintain and improve Performance, Security, Accessibility, and Usability. All Definitions of Done must be fulfilled with proper documentation of adjustments and collaboration with specialists.
"},{"auto_div_header":"
PI 28
Feature Catalog · Roadmap Initiative
Enhance Credit Interest Calculation to include unallocated payment segments
Penalty & Interest Enhancements · Core Revenue Management Capabilities
2986.9
Total Hours
1
Epic
GTM
Requester
Netcompany
","auto_div_body":"
Why It's Important
01
Accurate Interest Calculation
Enhance credit interest calculation to include unallocated payment segments, so as to provide an accurate interest calculation for all taxpayer payments.
02
Fair Taxpayer Treatment
Ensures taxpayers receive proper interest on all credit amounts, including unallocated payment segments that result from liability amendments and payment processing scenarios.
03
System Compliance
Update existing CREDIT_INT PI rule to calculate reimbursement interest on credit unallocated payment segments for tax account scenarios across implementations.
Key Epics & Features
Penalty & Interest Enhancements
Update calculation logic of CREDIT_INT (Reimbursement Interest Rule) to include credit unallocated payment segments next to credits on bill segments that is already implemented.
Payment Suspense Scenarios
Handle cases where invalid reference numbers result in payments that cannot be allocated to a specific party role, ensuring interest accrual on suspended amounts.
Payment File Processing Errors
Address automated payment file processing errors due to corrupted payment data or reference number mismatch, with proper interest calculation on unallocated segments.
Liability Amendment Handling
Process scenarios where liability amendments occur (e.g., reducing VAT liability), resulting in credit unallocated payment segments that must accrue interest in favor of taxpayer.
Payment Event State Transitions
Manage re-opening of completed payment events where original segments need cancellation or payment event re-distribution scenarios requiring segment reallocation with interest adjustments.
CREDIT_INT Rule Documentation
Document all adjustments for penalty and interest rule CREDIT_INT including performance, security, traceability, accessibility, and usability non-functional requirements.
"},{"auto_div_header":"
PI 28
Feature Catalog · Roadmap Initiative
Registration
Core Revenue Management Capabilities · Solon Tax 2026
2538.9
Total Hours
3
Epics
GRC
Requester
Netcompany
","auto_div_body":"
Why It's Important
01
Enhanced Validity Period Management
Enables revenue officers to complete multiple Party details changes on the same date while maintaining data integrity through improved uniqueness and overlapping checks for valid-from/valid-to periods.
02
Revenue Type Segmentation
Provides detailed revenue type categorization with Revenue Domain and Declaration Mechanism fields, enabling better segmentation for flows and GL decision table generation.
03
Organizational Hierarchy Visualization
Delivers graphical tree representations of organizational structures and taxpayer hierarchies, improving navigation efficiency and reducing misrouting across revenue departments.
Key Epics & Features
Enhance Validity Period Conditions in Registry Detail Entities
Support multiple modifications in the same date for Party and Party Role child entities including party_identifier, party_name, party_state, party_address, party_contact, bank_account, preferences, party_classification, party_additional_details, party_role_relationship, asset_party_role_relationship, asset_to_asset_relationship, valuation, and party_role_eligible_states. Introduces Valid State and State Reason fields with comprehensive uniqueness validation rules.
More Details Availability in Revenue Types
Adds optional fields for Revenue Domain and Declaration Mechanism to Revenue Type entities. Enables searching by Actively used status, Revenue Category, Revenue Type class, Priority, Revenue Domain, Declaration Mechanism, calendars, and related Claimants. Supports backend access to Revenue Type data for GL decision table generation.
View Organizational Structure and Taxpayers Hierarchy Structure
Provides graphical tree representations for organizational units, taxpayers (parties), and assets. Enables parent entity linking in list views and detail pages, hierarchical searching, and permission-based editing of parent relationships in entity history.
Validity Period Field Implementation
Implements searchable, visible, and updatable Valid State and State Reason fields across all Party child objects and Party Role entities with default valid_state=true and migration of existing records.
Uniqueness Validation Rules Engine
Enforces comprehensive uniqueness checks based on valid state flags, overlapping period detection, same-day change support, and primary record validation with proper handling of null validity dates.
Hierarchy Navigation and Search
Enables users to search by parent organizational units, parent parties, and parent assets with optional parent column visibility in list views and tree-based hierarchy exploration through modal interfaces.
"},{"auto_div_header":"
PI 28
Feature Catalog · Roadmap Initiative
Support Tax Account Overview Page in the Single Taxpayer Overview
Core Revenue Management Capabilities · Tax Accounts
1045.9
Total Hours
350
Estimate (Hours)
GTM
Requester
Netcompany
","auto_div_body":"
Why It's Important
01
Consolidated Financial View
Provides Revenue Authority users with a clear, consolidated view of all financial activities within a specific reconciliation period for a taxpayer's Tax Account, improving efficiency and accuracy.
02
Period-Centric Overview
Aggregates and presents all financial transactions related to selected reconciliation periods for a given revenue type registration, providing an immediate consolidated view of that period's financial status.
03
Enhanced Visibility
Bridges the gap in Solon Tax by introducing a dedicated user interface that presents Tax Account information in a single, easily accessible view for improved visibility and efficiency.
Key Epics & Features
Tax Account Overview Navigation
New redirect link on Revenue Type Registration page visible only for RTRs configured as Tax Account, navigating users to the Tax Account Overview page with current RTR context and selected reconciliation period.
Reconciliation Period Filtering
Users can filter financial transactions by reconciliation period with configurable From and To dates, supporting selection of multiple reconciliation periods for comprehensive period analysis.
Financial Transactions Table
Read-only table displaying all Financial Transactions linked to selected reconciliation period with opening and closing balances, supporting sorting, pagination, and Column Configurator functionality.
Opening and Closing Balance Calculation
Opening Balance equals closing balance of previous period; Closing Balance equals Opening plus all financial transactions including debts, payments, interests, write-offs, and refunds for the period.
Update Penalty & Interest Button
Users can access and select the Update P&I button to enter RECALCULATEFROMDATE parameter for running Penalty & Interest calculations on the Tax Account.
Comprehensive Transaction Columns
Table includes Creation Date, Effective Date, Accounting Date, Origin, FT ID, Revenue Type, FT Type, Posting Reason, Liability Balance, and Running Balance for complete financial transaction visibility.
"},{"auto_div_header":"
PI 28
Feature Catalog · Roadmap Initiative
Taxpayer Accounting
Core Revenue Management Capabilities · Solon Tax 2026
4479.3
Total Hours
3
Epics

Why It's Important
01
Enhanced Party Role Support
Enable financial event logging under Organization Units and Claimants as party roles, supporting offset vouchers, departmental transfers, and third-party settlements.
02
GL Decision Table Optimization
Reduce GL decision table complexity through multiline rules and additional parameters, decreasing FT types required and improving system maintainability.
03
High-Volume Rule Support
Support 200,000+ GL decision lines through configurable splitting across multiple sheets and files, enabling scalable handling of complex accounting rules.
Key Epics & Features
Enable Financial Events Logging under Org Units and Claimants
Support logging of revenue type registrations, obligations, segments, and financial transactions under Organization Units and Claimants as party roles with balance visibility and party role overview pages.
GL Decisions Table - Multiline Rules and Additional Parameters
Introduce Valid From/To columns, multiline support with amount splitting rules, and extensible parameters for claimant, revenue type, and segment categorization to reduce GL decision table complexity.
GL Decisions Table - Extensibility and High Volume Support
Enable rule-driven splitting of GL decision tables across multiple XLSX sheets and files, support custom adapters for journal entry creation, and maintain performance for 200,000+ decision lines.
Revenue Type Administration for Party Roles
Allow administrators to define revenue types for use with Organization Units and Claimants, enabling flexible party role relationships and financial transaction categorization.
GL Decision Table Configuration and Documentation
Provide comprehensive documentation for configuration options, split strategies, reference implementations, and operational guidelines ensuring maintainability and admin usability.
Performance, Security, and Accessibility Standards
Maintain performance for large datasets and multi-sheet evaluation, ensure no exposure of sensitive configuration data, and keep configuration screens and documentation accessible.
